steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Both mountain ranges in the notes share the 'sky islands' classification — isolated ranges whose environments differ from surrounding lowlands. The question asks for a sentence that emphasizes what the two ranges share, not how they differ.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'mentions multiple ranges generically': broadens to mountain ranges in general rather than focusing on these two specific ranges and what they share. ✗
  • Choice B — 'both are isolated ranges with distinct environments': makes the main clause about the shared classification — both are isolated mountain ranges whose environments differ from their surroundings — which is exactly the unity the notes establish. ✓
  • Choice C — 'emphasizes size difference': foregrounds the contrast in area (1,038 vs. 65 mi²) rather than the shared category. ✗
  • Choice D — 'pivots to location difference': acknowledges the shared label but then makes the main point a difference in location, undermining the unity the question asks for. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
